title: calocarpum procerum
intro: Calocarpum procerum, also known as the West African calocarpum, is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Calocarpum procerum are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Calocarpum procerum can be made into teas, decoctions, or infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
